title: SDK keepalive misuse triggering proxy handshake storms and graceful reject shim
preview:
Summary:
Customers reported recurring 5xx waves and long tail latency spikes during high-volume websocket-to-rest fallback traffic. We traced root cause to abusive SDK keepalive/short-lived connection patterns that trigger repeated TLS renegotiation at our edge proxies. Those renegotiations exhaust proxy worker thre...
text:
Summary:
Customers reported recurring 5xx waves and long tail latency spikes during high-volume websocket-to-rest fallback traffic. We traced root cause to abusive SDK keepalive/short-lived connection patterns that trigger repeated TLS renegotiation at our edge proxies. Those renegotiations exhaust proxy worker threads, causing queued requests, connection resets, and client retries which amplify the failure into tenant-visible 502/504 waves.

Why this is unique: Unlike previous incidents that were primarily backpressure or KV cache related, this issue is a connection-management pathology driven by (a) an SDK pattern that reopened connections in <2s cycles and (b) our proxy TLS session handling which did a heavy handshake path per reconnection. The combination produces a handshake storm during tenant surges and a correlated retry amplification across the hosted API surface.

Scope & Im...

title: SDK quickstart & localized sample catalog for streaming + function-calling + structured output
preview: Goal: deliver a curated quickstart and localized sample catalog demonstrating streaming responses, function/tool calling, and structured output across the SDKs (Python, Node, Go). The kit should include: (1) three minimal quickstarts (streaming chat with function calls, structured JSON responses for form extraction,...
text:
Goal: deliver a curated quickstart and localized sample catalog demonstrating streaming responses, function/tool calling, and structured output across the SDKs (Python, Node, Go). The kit should include: (1) three minimal quickstarts (streaming chat with function calls, structured JSON responses for form extraction, and streaming embeddings pipeline), (2) localized README translations for en, fr, de, ja, and (3) a sample verification CI that runs smoke tests and validates structured schema outputs. This ticket coordinates PM, Docs, SDK Eng, Design, and Localization to produce publish-ready artifacts and release notes entries.

Scope: author sample code, write short HOWTOs for each platform, create a release-notes matrix tying sample files to feature flags and API changes, design lightweight diagrams for console docs, onboard translations, and add telemetry checks to telemetry-dashboar...

title: Roadmap Orchestra playbook for cross-team dependency weeks
preview: Create an operational playbook (the 'Orchestra') to run recurring 1-week dependency windows around major roadmap changes. This ticket captures the scope, roles, comms, and checklist for two objectives: 1) reduce cross-team unblock time during critical rollouts, and 2) produce an auditable handoff for Exec/Board read...
text: Create an operational playbook (the 'Orchestra') to run recurring 1-week dependency windows around major roadmap changes. This ticket captures the scope, roles, comms, and checklist for two objectives: 1) reduce cross-team unblock time during critical rollouts, and 2) produce an auditable handoff for Exec/Board readouts and post-rollout retros. The playbook standardizes pre-window validation, live coordination, and post-window regression checks so that PM, Eng, and Design have a shared cadence for complex initiative merges and infra migrations.

title: Client adapter contract: resumable iterators, idempotent paging, and streaming backpressure
preview: Define a single, language-idiomatic contract and acceptance criteria for paginated fetches, resumable iterators, retry semantics, and streaming APIs across Python, TypeScript, and Go SDKs. The goal is to enable zero-downtime client upgrades and predictable developer expectations when switching runtime models or serv...
text: Define a single, language-idiomatic contract and acceptance criteria for paginated fetches, resumable iterators, retry semantics, and streaming APIs across Python, TypeScript, and Go SDKs. The goal is to enable zero-downtime client upgrades and predictable developer expectations when switching runtime models or server fallbacks.

title: Adaptive toggle evolution & client migration kit for streaming/tool-call structured outputs
preview: Purpose: Provide a prescriptive migration kit and operational play for rolling behavioral changes that affect streaming, tool/function calling, and structured outputs. The kit must minimize customer breakage, accelerate client SDK migrations, and provide reproducible backcompat tests that exercise streaming+toolcall...
text:
Purpose: Provide a prescriptive migration kit and operational play for rolling behavioral changes that affect streaming, tool/function calling, and structured outputs. The kit must minimize customer breakage, accelerate client SDK migrations, and provide reproducible backcompat tests that exercise streaming+toolcall edge-cases.

Context: We are introducing a set of behavior flag changes to standardize structured-output schema negotiation and to fix long-standing streaming termination edge cases that cause downstream tool-call parsers to mis-interpret partial chunks. These changes improve correctness and long-tail cost, but risk client regressions in older SDKs and bespoke tool integrations. This ticket is the PM-facing orchestration: docs, migration guide, staged deprecation timeline, success signals, and the required acceptance criteria for engineering to implement the hooks and test...

title: Inkwell ingest gateway and schema fallback coordinator
preview: Goal: Build 'Inkwell', a resilient ingest gateway that performs probabilistic schema scoring, lineage annotation, and coordinates an automatic schema-fallback path to preserve customers' console analytics when upstream producers are noisy or schema-evolving. Scope: (1) a streaming pre-processor that scores events ag...
text: Goal: Build 'Inkwell', a resilient ingest gateway that performs probabilistic schema scoring, lineage annotation, and coordinates an automatic schema-fallback path to preserve customers' console analytics when upstream producers are noisy or schema-evolving. Scope: (1) a streaming pre-processor that scores events against the active contract using a lightweight probabilistic scorer (fast decision path) and tags events with a schema_confidence score; (2) a small nearline repair pipeline (repair.shim) that attempts minimal coercions for low-confidence events and emits repaired copies to the main pipeline; (3) a fallback coordinator which, under sustained contract drift, routes events to a permissive "compat" topic and surfaces a summarized mutation batch to the contract registry for review; (4) operator-facing metrics, traces, and a canary rollout plan. Acceptance criteria: (A) end-to-en...

title: Real-world client-mix streaming harness with paced tokenflow and p95 regression gate
preview: Goal: build a load-testing harness that emulates a realistic mix of streaming chat clients (SSE + WebSocket) with heterogeneous token pacing, intermittent connection churn, and per-tenant KV-cache warm/cold states. The tool should focus on producing stable tokens/sec curves, p95 end-to-end latency tracking for first...
text:
Goal: build a load-testing harness that emulates a realistic mix of streaming chat clients (SSE + WebSocket) with heterogeneous token pacing, intermittent connection churn, and per-tenant KV-cache warm/cold states. The tool should focus on producing stable tokens/sec curves, p95 end-to-end latency tracking for first-byte and last-byte, and automated regression detection with signal decomposition (cold-cache vs. warm-cache vs. network variance).

Why: Existing harnesses produce high-level tokenflow numbers but fail to reproduce real-world client diversity (mobile vs. desktop, slow vs. aggressive token consumption, backgrounded clients that resume). This causes missed regressions where an optimization improves median throughput but regresses p95 for low-throughput long-tail clients.

Scope: implement a harness and analysis pipeline that can: (1) generate client mixes with configurable d...

title: Dynamic runbook injection and PagerDuty context enrichment for traced incidents
preview: Problem: When high-severity incidents fire, responders spend time gathering context (top traces, correlated logs/metrics, relevant dashboards) and manually selecting runbooks. This increases MTTR and causes inconsistent postmortems. Goal: Implement a system that automatically enriches PagerDuty incidents and Slack a...
text: Problem: When high-severity incidents fire, responders spend time gathering context (top traces, correlated logs/metrics, relevant dashboards) and manually selecting runbooks. This increases MTTR and causes inconsistent postmortems. Goal: Implement a system that automatically enriches PagerDuty incidents and Slack alert threads with curated incident context (trace links, top correlated metrics/log snippets), and injects a suggested, editable runbook into the incident and Slack thread. Additionally seed a draft postmortem with an initial timeline generated from traces and critical logs to accelerate RCA.

title: Eviction-aware striped KV allocation for asymmetric tensor/pipeline shards
preview: Background: In multi-GPU serving with tensor and pipeline parallelism, KV cache memory pressure is highly asymmetric: some pipeline stages / tensor shards accumulate large KV segments for long contexts while others remain underutilized. Continuous batching exacerbates this because request sequence-length skew and ba...
text:
Background: In multi-GPU serving with tensor and pipeline parallelism, KV cache memory pressure is highly asymmetric: some pipeline stages / tensor shards accumulate large KV segments for long contexts while others remain underutilized. Continuous batching exacerbates this because request sequence-length skew and batching windows change per-step, leading to transient OOMs or suboptimal conservative reservations.

Goal: Design and implement an eviction-aware striped allocation strategy for the KV cache that: 1) shards KV storage into fine-grained "stripes" spread across GPUs, 2) supports local eviction/compaction policies to reclaim memory without global pauses, 3) selectively replicates small hot entries to reduce cross-shard RPCs, and 4) integrates with the serving runtime's continuous batching and routing logic to minimize tail latency and avoid OOMs.

Approach overview:
- Stripe la...

title: Gateway burst-window parsing regression caused 429 wave — follow-up & remediation
preview: Summary: On 2026-02-25 15:12 UTC a configuration parsing change deployed to the API gateway caused the per-tenant burst window field (burst_window_ms) to be interpreted as seconds instead of milliseconds. This produced a 1000x reduction in effective burst window for many customers, rapidly triggering quota enforceme...
text: Summary: On 2026-02-25 15:12 UTC a configuration parsing change deployed to the API gateway caused the per-tenant burst window field (burst_window_ms) to be interpreted as seconds instead of milliseconds. This produced a 1000x reduction in effective burst window for many customers, rapidly triggering quota enforcement and producing a sustained 429 storm across multiple regions for ~45 minutes. The incident impacted request success rates for hosted API customers and triggered automated retries that amplified load on internal services.

title: Batch-state preemption corruption causing intermittent token duplication and p95 latency spikes
preview: Summary: During a regional capacity reclaim event (preemption cycle) the serving runtime's batching state occasionally became inconsistent between the scheduler and worker kernels. This manifested as duplicated tokens in responses for a subset of requests and transient p95/p99 latency spikes on affected hosts. The b...
text: Summary: During a regional capacity reclaim event (preemption cycle) the serving runtime's batching state occasionally became inconsistent between the scheduler and worker kernels. This manifested as duplicated tokens in responses for a subset of requests and transient p95/p99 latency spikes on affected hosts. The behavior appears when preemption interrupts an in-flight batch while KV-cache warmup and kernel handoff are occurring. Impact: Intermittent user-facing incorrect token output and latency regressions for ~0.8% of requests on impacted nodes over a 6-hour window. Scope: Hosted API customers in region us-west-2 on runtime-1.22 serving certain GPT-style models with mixed-precision kernels and autoscaling enabled.

title: Customer-fidelity capture profiles & stability harness for Private/VPC workloads
preview: Build a reproducible harness that captures lightweight customer workload profiles inside Private / VPC environments and uses them to run stability and regression benchmarks on on-prem and VPC deployments. The harness should produce token/sec and p95 latency time series, support controlled noise injection (network, C...
text: Build a reproducible harness that captures lightweight customer workload profiles inside Private / VPC environments and uses them to run stability and regression benchmarks on on-prem and VPC deployments. The harness should produce token/sec and p95 latency time series, support controlled noise injection (network, CPU jitter), and run nightly diff jobs that surface regressions with automated triage hints.

title: Prefill boundary detector and microbenchmark swap policy for safer op-choice transitions
preview: Problem: For mixed prefill/decode traffic we observe cases where switching to a lower-latency kernel mid-request (typically after a prefill boundary) reduces tail latency but can regress correctness or spike p99 when KV warming and memory layout expectations differ. Current heuristics are brittle across architecture...
text:
Problem: For mixed prefill/decode traffic we observe cases where switching to a lower-latency kernel mid-request (typically after a prefill boundary) reduces tail latency but can regress correctness or spike p99 when KV warming and memory layout expectations differ. Current heuristics are brittle across architectures and sequence-length distributions.

Proposal: Implement a two-part runtime subsystem: (1) a lightweight prefill-boundary detector that tags requests where the runtime can safely attempt an op-variant swap after prefill (based on token-phase, recent seqlen histogram, and active KV cache state), and (2) a background microbenchmarking service that continuously produces per-(model,arch,seq-len-bucket) swap safety scores derived from short, sandboxed probes. The runtime will consult both signals to decide a soft-swap policy: allow trial switches for a small probe budget, monit...

title: Hybrid-Minute Reservation Bands, Soft-Preempt Policy, and SLA Tier Pilot
preview:
Context: Enterprise customers request more granular reserved capacity options to better match variable workloads (short bursts and minute-level spikes) without paying for long-duration commitments. Current committed-hour SKU is too coarse for many ML infra patterns (jobs with 1-30 minute peaks).

Hypothesis: Introdu...
text:
Context: Enterprise customers request more granular reserved capacity options to better match variable workloads (short bursts and minute-level spikes) without paying for long-duration commitments. Current committed-hour SKU is too coarse for many ML infra patterns (jobs with 1-30 minute peaks).

Hypothesis: Introducing minute-level reservation bands (e.g., 15, 60, 180 minute commit bands) combined with a soft-preempt policy and a small overage credit pool will increase conversion from trial to committed customers by 8-12% among mid-market accounts and reduce short-term overprovision requests, while preserving predictable revenue.

Proposal: Run a 12-week pilot offering a new SKU family: Hybrid-Minute. Key elements:
- Reservation bands sold as bundles of guaranteed-minutes per month (e.g., 10k x 15m blocks, 4k x 60m blocks).
- Soft-preempt policy: when a reserved minute is consumed, t...

title: Adaptive Tempo Buckets: cadence-aware roadmap scorer for inference economics
preview: Problem: Standard RICE scoring treats effort and impact as static inputs; for inference platform work we see strong time-to-value and capacity-regime effects (short fast wins vs long platform bets), and token-cost/latency sensitivity that varies by workload. Goal: design a practical, repeatable prioritization model ...
text: Problem: Standard RICE scoring treats effort and impact as static inputs; for inference platform work we see strong time-to-value and capacity-regime effects (short fast wins vs long platform bets), and token-cost/latency sensitivity that varies by workload. Goal: design a practical, repeatable prioritization model that (a) augments RICE with a tempo dimension, (b) applies workload-aware multipliers for token economics and latency risk, and (c) is cycle-aware so scores decay/refresh with cadence. Deliverables: 1) Scoring rubric and spreadsheet prototype (tempo buckets: Sprint, Quarter, Strategic) combining Reach/Impact/Confidence/Effort with Tempo weight and Econ multipliers; 2) Example scoring for 12 candidate initiatives across inference, dedicated, and console; 3) Implementation guidance for PMs and Intake form changes; 4) Small pilot in next cycle to validate alignment with capaci...

title: Observability Sanctuary: keyed TTL + just-in-time unmasking for sensitive telemetry
preview: Implement a tenant-aware "Sanctuary" layer for observability streams that enforces keyed TTLs, encrypted salted identifiers, and a just-in-time unmasking workflow for authorized audits. The feature targets traces, error logs, and latency telemetry and must integrate with RBAC and the audit ledger so reveal actions a...
text: Implement a tenant-aware "Sanctuary" layer for observability streams that enforces keyed TTLs, encrypted salted identifiers, and a just-in-time unmasking workflow for authorized audits. The feature targets traces, error logs, and latency telemetry and must integrate with RBAC and the audit ledger so reveal actions are recorded with a proof token.

title: SLA-driven circuit breaker and staged backpressure for console metrics ingest
preview: Problem: spikes in customer console traffic (bulk exports, aggressive dashboards) are occasionally violating downstream SLOs for per-route P95 latency and causing downstream processors to OOM or fall into retry storms. Existing ingest admission is binary (accept/reject) and causes sharp client-side errors and noisy ...
text: Problem: spikes in customer console traffic (bulk exports, aggressive dashboards) are occasionally violating downstream SLOs for per-route P95 latency and causing downstream processors to OOM or fall into retry storms. Existing ingest admission is binary (accept/reject) and causes sharp client-side errors and noisy retries. Goal: build an SLA-driven circuit-breaker and staged backpressure system in the realtime metrics ingest pipeline that can: (1) slow or shed low-value events, (2) gracefully degrade to sampling mode, (3) signal back upstream with clear retry-after semantics, and (4) automatically recover when capacity returns. This ticket covers design, prototype implementation in the ingress proxy + stream preprocessor, and a controlled rollout to select tenants.

title: Dedicated capacity performance gate and load lab
preview: Establish a repeatable lab and gate for measuring dedicated capacity throughput and for generating customer-facing acceptance artifacts. Focus on consistent measurement semantics, traffic shaping, and pass/fail criteria that map to committed throughput SLAs.
text: Establish a repeatable lab and gate for measuring dedicated capacity throughput and for generating customer-facing acceptance artifacts. Focus on consistent measurement semantics, traffic shaping, and pass/fail criteria that map to committed throughput SLAs.

title: Microbatch Accordion Binner with Kernel Hinting and Prefill-aware Trimming
preview: Goal: Reduce padding waste and CPU/GPU kernel mismatch penalties for diverse sequence-length workloads by introducing an 'accordion' bucketing strategy that dynamically resizes buckets at runtime and surfaces kernel-hint metadata to the scheduler. Background: Current static bucketing and length-based coalescers stil...
text: Goal: Reduce padding waste and CPU/GPU kernel mismatch penalties for diverse sequence-length workloads by introducing an 'accordion' bucketing strategy that dynamically resizes buckets at runtime and surfaces kernel-hint metadata to the scheduler. Background: Current static bucketing and length-based coalescers still suffer in high-variance traffic (chatty short messages mixed with long contexts) — we pay token and kernel inefficiency (suboptimal kernel selection, poor occupancy) when many microbatches pad to long seqlen. Approach: 1) Accordion Buckets: buckets are parameterized by a compressibility factor; short-heavy tails cause nearby buckets to collapse (narrow width) while long-tail regions expand, creating an accordion-like histogram that adapts every N seconds. 2) Kernel Hint Score: each incoming request computes a lightweight kernel hint (int8 score) indicating preferred kerne...

title: Quantile Warranty & Autoscale Coordination for Dedicated Serve Capacity
preview: Problem statement: enterprise customers need clear, measurable guarantees for dedicated GPU pools. Current SLA drafts use single-point targets (e.g., tokens/min) and simple credit tables that don't reflect bursty real-world traffic or staggered degradation. This ticket defines a quantile-driven warranty (p95/p99 win...
text:
Problem statement: enterprise customers need clear, measurable guarantees for dedicated GPU pools. Current SLA drafts use single-point targets (e.g., tokens/min) and simple credit tables that don't reflect bursty real-world traffic or staggered degradation. This ticket defines a quantile-driven warranty (p95/p99 windows + AUC degradation) and ties it to autoscaler interlocks, customer notices, and contract crediting language.

Scope:
- Define measurable SLA metrics (quantiles, sustained shortfalls, AUC-based crediting).
- Specify autoscaler guardrails and interlock logic to avoid oscillation between autoscale and remediation credits.
- Draft customer-facing contract clauses (notice windows, remediation steps, credit computation appendix).
- Produce monitoring dashboards and alerting thresholds needed to support legal measurement.

Out of scope: per-tenant billing/credits plumbing and ...

title: Operator Rate Sculptor — Preset Chains, Emergency Holds, and Provenance Timeline
preview: This ticket scopes the Console UI feature 'Operator Rate Sculptor' — a set of fine-grained fleet controls for Dedicated capacity customers that lets operators create chained scaling presets, place emergency holds (throttle/freeze), and inspect a provenance timeline for every control change. The goal is to provide de...
text:
This ticket scopes the Console UI feature 'Operator Rate Sculptor' — a set of fine-grained fleet controls for Dedicated capacity customers that lets operators create chained scaling presets, place emergency holds (throttle/freeze), and inspect a provenance timeline for every control change. The goal is to provide deterministic throughput guarantees while giving SRE/operators clear rollback and auditability instruments.

Problem statement:
- Operators currently lack a single surfaced workflow to create repeatable scaling presets and to temporarily throttle fleets during incidents.
- Change history is fragmented across infra logs and audit tables; Console needs an integrated provenance timeline with context (who, why, linked runbooks/PRs).

This ticket covers UX flows, acceptance criteria, and a phased rollout with toggles for enterprise customers.

title: Geo-Request Classification Engine and SLA-Aware Routing for Enterprise Onboarding
preview: Launch a classification + routing service that automatically triages enterprise region/residency requests into a prioritized intake queue and assigns an SLA-aware routing policy (e.g., local-region, cross-border with contractual exceptions, failover-only). The engine will consume customer intake forms, contract cons...
text: Launch a classification + routing service that automatically triages enterprise region/residency requests into a prioritized intake queue and assigns an SLA-aware routing policy (e.g., local-region, cross-border with contractual exceptions, failover-only). The engine will consume customer intake forms, contract constraints, and operational capacity signals to recommend: priority bucket, target region(s), failover mapping, estimated provisioning lead time, and a minimal set of compliance controls required. This will reduce manual adjudication overhead for Sales/RevOps/Onboarding and improve time-to-provision for high-value customers.

title: Throughput contracting and adaptive autoscale protocol for Dedicated capacity
preview: Create a repeatable provisioning and approval protocol that contracts throughput commitments, binds autoscaling policy templates, and sets fleet-level controls for Dedicated capacity. The deliverable is a product playbook + flow automations in Console that operators and sales can use to intake reservation requests, ...
text: Create a repeatable provisioning and approval protocol that contracts throughput commitments, binds autoscaling policy templates, and sets fleet-level controls for Dedicated capacity. The deliverable is a product playbook + flow automations in Console that operators and sales can use to intake reservation requests, validate SLA targets, and produce a binding capacity agreement with clear rollback/fallback behavior.

title: Jurisdictional geofence routing with tiered fallback model variants and allow/deny overrides
preview: Summary: Implement a jurisdictional geofence routing layer that enforces customer-configurable residency and country-level allowlists/denylists, supports tier-aware route selection (hosted/dedicated/private), and performs deterministic fallback to privacy-preserving model variants when hard routing cannot be satisfi...
text:
Summary: Implement a jurisdictional geofence routing layer that enforces customer-configurable residency and country-level allowlists/denylists, supports tier-aware route selection (hosted/dedicated/private), and performs deterministic fallback to privacy-preserving model variants when hard routing cannot be satisfied. This ticket covers runtime enforcement points, control-plane policy schema, audit logging, and a staged rollout plan for EU/Schengen compliance customers.

Background: Customers in regulated regions require both strict data residency guarantees and the ability to opt their requests into or out of specific jurisdictions. Current routing primitives (region hints + simple region denylists) are insufficient for operator-defined allow/deny policies, tier-aware capacity constraints, and predictable graceful degradation when regional capacity is saturated. Legal and Sales have...

title: Cross-Variant Tooling Parity Scorer and Ops Runbook
preview: Build a lightweight, automated compatibility scorer and operational runbook that grades model variants for tokenizer/context/tooling parity and ties score thresholds into smart routing policies. This ticket creates: (1) a parity 'scorecard' that quantifies tokenizer and KV/cache/context compatibility across model va...
text: Build a lightweight, automated compatibility scorer and operational runbook that grades model variants for tokenizer/context/tooling parity and ties score thresholds into smart routing policies. This ticket creates: (1) a parity 'scorecard' that quantifies tokenizer and KV/cache/context compatibility across model variants; (2) a nightly scanner that updates the scorecard and emits metrics; (3) an ops runbook and gating guidance for automating fallback and for manual remediation when parity drops below thresholds.

title: Epochal batch quietude: slot lockups, priority inversion and remediation playbook
preview: Summary: On 2026-02-19 we observed repeated multi-minute periods where continuous batch formation across a subset of serving pods dropped to near-zero ('quietude'), producing tail latency spikes for latency-sensitive customers and an apparent unfairness where lower-priority tenants consumed disproportionate slots af...
text: Summary: On 2026-02-19 we observed repeated multi-minute periods where continuous batch formation across a subset of serving pods dropped to near-zero ('quietude'), producing tail latency spikes for latency-sensitive customers and an apparent unfairness where lower-priority tenants consumed disproportionate slots after the quiet window. This ticket captures the incident drive, root cause analysis, immediate mitigations, and a concrete playbook of action items to prevent recurrence.

title: Runtime <-> Simulator drift causing allowance calculation mismatch
preview: Summary: We discovered a reproducible discrepancy between the decisions produced by the offline rate-policy simulator (used for QA and rollout validation) and the live runtime evaluator. The simulator reports identical traffic as ALLOW while the runtime occasionally DENYs the same request contexts under identical in...
text: Summary: We discovered a reproducible discrepancy between the decisions produced by the offline rate-policy simulator (used for QA and rollout validation) and the live runtime evaluator. The simulator reports identical traffic as ALLOW while the runtime occasionally DENYs the same request contexts under identical inputs. This causes confidence drift for policy rollouts and has caused two near-miss incidents where canary traffic was incorrectly classified as safe to increase capacity. Root cause appears to be a small normalization mismatch and different tie-breaking logic between the simulator's policy preprocessor and the runtime policy engine.

title: Batching + KV-cache aliasing causing silent accuracy drift: mitigation, canary expansion, and rollout plan
preview: Summary: During a production capacity scale event on 2026-02-09 we observed a gradual accuracy drift for a small set of quantized 8-bit model variants serving chat completions. The drift manifested as higher semantic hallucination rates and several customer-reported classification errors. The incident did not cause ...
text:
Summary: During a production capacity scale event on 2026-02-09 we observed a gradual accuracy drift for a small set of quantized 8-bit model variants serving chat completions. The drift manifested as higher semantic hallucination rates and several customer-reported classification errors. The incident did not cause service-wide crash loops but produced correlated degraded outputs for multi-tenant workloads that used continuous batching.

What happened: Autoscaling and burst batching combined with the runtime's KV-cache prefetch path allowed requests from different model variants (same architecture but different quantization profiles) to reuse prefetched KV entries under certain race conditions. This KV aliasing resulted in silent state leakage across requests, biasing attention for subsequent tokens and producing subtle-but-measurable output drift. The bug is deterministic under a spe...

title: Retention + redaction rules for audit fields (PII/secret safety + residency behavior)
preview:
Goal
Implement and validate a consistent set of redaction + retention rules for audit log fields across Hosted/Dedicated/Private, ensuring we never log secrets and we minimize PII. This ticket covers:
- Field-level data classification + handling requirements for audit events (v1)
- Redaction/hashing patterns enforce...
text:
Goal
Implement and validate a consistent set of redaction + retention rules for audit log fields across Hosted/Dedicated/Private, ensuring we never log secrets and we minimize PII. This ticket covers:
- Field-level data classification + handling requirements for audit events (v1)
- Redaction/hashing patterns enforced in producers and exporters
- Residency/retention behavior documented and verified (what stays in-region, what gets exported, TTLs)
- Tests + CI guardrails to prevent regressions (secrets-in-logs)

Why (customer + compliance)
- Enterprise security reviews are asking for explicit statements on what we log, how we avoid secrets/PII, and how long audit logs are retained (and where). This blocks Private deals.
- SOC2/ISO evidence expects: auditable actions recorded, restricted access, tamper-resistant-ish pipeline, retention policy, and privacy-safe logging.

References / arti...
tags: Audit logging standardization (schema v1 + 100% sensitive actions coverage)

title: Policy engine: emit v1 audit events for routing/policy changes (fallback rules, allow-lists) with diff + approver context
preview:
Problem
Policy/routing changes are classified as “sensitive actions” for SOC2/ISO-aligned auditability and customer incident investigations. Today policy-service emits partial/legacy logs and does not consistently capture (a) canonical event_type, (b) stable diff summaries, and (c) approver context when changes go t...
text:
Problem
Policy/routing changes are classified as “sensitive actions” for SOC2/ISO-aligned auditability and customer incident investigations. Today policy-service emits partial/legacy logs and does not consistently capture (a) canonical event_type, (b) stable diff summaries, and (c) approver context when changes go through console workflows.

Scope (in-scope)
- Emit schema v1 audit events for policy/routing config mutations across Hosted/Dedicated/Private:
 - Fallback rules updates (e.g., primary model -> fallback model variants, region failover behavior)
 - Allow-list / deny-list updates (e.g., model allow-list by tenant/project, routing constraints)
 - Policy set updates (create/update/delete of policy objects; enable/disable)
- Include a diff summary suitable for SIEM ingestion (bounded cardinality, no secrets/PII).
- Include “approver context” where applicable (console-driven or AP...
tags: Audit Logging Standardization (Schema v1 + Sensitive Action Coverage)

title: Publish audit logging docs + runbooks; verify alert/dashboard links and incident templates
preview:
Goal
Finalize and publish the audit logging documentation set and operational runbooks, then validate that all dashboards/alerts and incident workflows link to the correct sources. This is the “last mile” for enterprise readiness: we need consistent internal guidance (SRE + Support + Solutions) and externally sharea...
text:
Goal
Finalize and publish the audit logging documentation set and operational runbooks, then validate that all dashboards/alerts and incident workflows link to the correct sources. This is the “last mile” for enterprise readiness: we need consistent internal guidance (SRE + Support + Solutions) and externally shareable references (schema, delivery semantics, SIEM export guidance) without making roadmap promises.

Scope
- Confluence publish pass: schema, taxonomy, versioning/backcompat, redaction/data classification, logging standard.
- Runbooks publish pass: audit pipeline incident runbook + coverage regression runbook.
- Verify links from:
 - observability dashboards/alerts (drop rate / latency / backlog, coverage dashboard)
 - on-call dashboard widgets
 - incident templates / postmortem templates (where we reference audit pipeline)
- Internal enablement:
 - short “what changed” note...
tags: Audit Logging Standardization (Schema v1 + Sensitive Actions Coverage)

title: Console backend: emit v1 audit events for admin actions with consistent actor attribution
preview:
Problem
Console (web) admin workflows currently trigger sensitive actions in multiple backend services (Console backend, Control Plane, Exporter config service). Today we have partial/legacy audit events and inconsistent attribution (sometimes only service identity, missing impersonation, missing source IP/user agen...
text:
Problem
Console (web) admin workflows currently trigger sensitive actions in multiple backend services (Console backend, Control Plane, Exporter config service). Today we have partial/legacy audit events and inconsistent attribution (sometimes only service identity, missing impersonation, missing source IP/user agent), which breaks investigation workflows and causes inconsistent SIEM fields (ref SUP-18477).

Goal
Ensure Console backend emits canonical Audit Event Schema v1 events for Console-initiated sensitive admin actions (role edits, audit log export config changes, retention/residency settings). Events must follow the v1 schema + taxonomy, and actor attribution must be consistent across Hosted/Dedicated/Private.

Scope (Console backend)
1) RBAC/admin access changes initiated via Console UI
- Roles: create/update/delete role; role permission edits
- Assignments: add/remove user to...

title: Add CI check: audit events must include required v1 fields (and registered event_type)
preview:
Context
- We’re standardizing audit logs across Hosted/Dedicated/Private with a canonical schema v1 + event taxonomy.
- Instrumentation work is underway service-by-service, but we keep regressing on required fields (missing source_ip, missing actor.id for console actions, missing request_id/trace_id, etc.).

text:
Context
- We’re standardizing audit logs across Hosted/Dedicated/Private with a canonical schema v1 + event taxonomy.
- Instrumentation work is underway service-by-service, but we keep regressing on required fields (missing source_ip, missing actor.id for console actions, missing request_id/trace_id, etc.).
- We need a CI gate that prevents:
 (a) emitting unregistered event_type values
 (b) emitting v1 events missing required fields for a given event_type
 (c) introducing new audited code paths without test coverage hooks (where feasible)

Goal
Add an enforcement step in CI for the monorepo that validates audit event emission sites against:
- the event_type registry (taxonomy)
- schema v1 required fields (global required + per-event required)
- redaction rules (basic pattern checks for secrets/PII where we can statically detect)

Non-goals
- Perfect static analysis across all language...
tags: Audit Logging: Schema v1 + Sensitive Actions Coverage

title: Add integration tests: sensitive actions emit v1 audit events (hosted + private fixtures)
preview:
Context
We are standardizing audit logging across Hosted/Dedicated/Private and gating launch on “100% coverage for sensitive actions.” Unit tests in individual services are not sufficient to prevent regressions (new endpoints / UI flows missing audit emission, missing required fields, inconsistent actor attribution)...
text:
Context
We are standardizing audit logging across Hosted/Dedicated/Private and gating launch on “100% coverage for sensitive actions.” Unit tests in individual services are not sufficient to prevent regressions (new endpoints / UI flows missing audit emission, missing required fields, inconsistent actor attribution). This ticket adds end-to-end tests that perform representative sensitive actions and assert that a correctly-shaped schema v1 audit event is produced.

Goals
- Catch missing audit events for sensitive actions during CI before merge.
- Validate schema v1 shape + required fields via the shared validator library.
- Validate actor/target semantics and correlation fields (request_id/trace_id/admin_action_id) for investigation workflows.
- Ensure parity: the same action in Hosted fixture and Private fixture yields an equivalent event_type + required fields.

Non-goals
- Building...
tags: Audit logging standardization (schema v1 + 100% sensitive actions coverage)

title: Audit event_type registry (enum) + build-time lints for schema-required fields
preview:
We need a centralized, canonical registry of audit event types (taxonomy) and enforcement so engineers cannot:
- emit an unregistered event_type
- omit required schema fields for a given event_type

This ticket delivers:
1) a single event_type registry (enum) aligned to the sensitive-actions taxonomy
2) build-time l...
text:
We need a centralized, canonical registry of audit event types (taxonomy) and enforcement so engineers cannot:
- emit an unregistered event_type
- omit required schema fields for a given event_type

This ticket delivers:
1) a single event_type registry (enum) aligned to the sensitive-actions taxonomy
2) build-time lint rules that fail CI when an event emitter uses an unknown event_type or misses required fields

This is a prerequisite for credible “100% coverage” claims and for keeping schema semantics stable across Hosted/Dedicated/Private.
tags: Audit Logging Standardization (Schema v1 + 100% Sensitive Actions Coverage)

title: Private installer: emit audit events for install/upgrade/rollback/config apply + credential rotation
preview:
Context
Private customers (esp. regulated) are blocking on “parity” for sensitive actions: they want the same audit semantics in Private deployments as Hosted/Dedicated. Today the Private installer/upgrade tooling performs critical actions (install, upgrade, rollback, config apply, credential rotation) but does not ...
text:
Context
Private customers (esp. regulated) are blocking on “parity” for sensitive actions: they want the same audit semantics in Private deployments as Hosted/Dedicated. Today the Private installer/upgrade tooling performs critical actions (install, upgrade, rollback, config apply, credential rotation) but does not emit consistently shaped audit events, making investigations and SOC2 evidence collection difficult.

Goal
Instrument the redwood-private-installer and related upgrade tooling to emit canonical audit events (schema v1) for the following sensitive operator actions:
1) Install
2) Upgrade
3) Rollback
4) Config apply (including changes to audit export destinations, retention/residency, auth/RBAC bootstrap where applicable)
5) Credential rotation (installer-managed creds/certs/tokens)

Scope
- Private installer CLI + any invoked “apply”/“migrate” steps that mutate cluster state....
tags: Audit logging standardization (schema v1 + 100% sensitive-action coverage)

title: Telemetry coverage dashboard for sensitive-action audit events (expected vs observed + alerts)
preview:
Goal
Build an internal coverage dashboard and alerting that verifies we are emitting audit events for 100% of “sensitive actions” across Hosted/Dedicated/Private. Dashboard must show expected event types vs observed event types, broken down by producer service and deployment mode. Add automated alerts for regression...
text:
Goal
Build an internal coverage dashboard and alerting that verifies we are emitting audit events for 100% of “sensitive actions” across Hosted/Dedicated/Private. Dashboard must show expected event types vs observed event types, broken down by producer service and deployment mode. Add automated alerts for regressions (missing or dropping events).

Why
- We need a repeatable, reviewable way to prove coverage (internal security/compliance + customer security reviews).
- We need regression detection: new endpoints/features frequently ship without audit emission.
- We need pipeline health separation from coverage (i.e., differentiate “service never emits” vs “sink is down”).

Scope (this ticket)
1) Coverage model + data source
- “Expected” set comes from the sensitive-actions event taxonomy registry (ENG-4822). Expected list includes:
 - event_type (canonical string)
 - required_dimension...
tags: Audit Logging Standardization (Schema v1 + 100% Sensitive Actions Coverage)

title: Migrate legacy audit events to v1 normalized schema (schema_version tagging + field normalization)
preview:
Context
- We have multiple legacy audit event producers across API Gateway, Control Plane, Console backend, and Private installer that emit inconsistent payload shapes and field names.
- Customers consuming via SIEM export (Splunk/Sentinel/etc.) are seeing mismatched fields across event types and releases, and dupli...
text:
Context
- We have multiple legacy audit event producers across API Gateway, Control Plane, Console backend, and Private installer that emit inconsistent payload shapes and field names.
- Customers consuming via SIEM export (Splunk/Sentinel/etc.) are seeing mismatched fields across event types and releases, and duplicates are harder to dedupe without stable event identifiers.
- This ticket is the migration plan + implementation coordination to ensure all legacy producers are normalized into the canonical v1 schema, with explicit schema_version tagging and a deprecation window for legacy fields.

Goals (what “done” means)
1) All audit events exported externally (and stored internally) conform to Audit Schema v1 OR are normalized to v1 at the exporter boundary.
2) Every exported event includes schema_version (e.g., "1.0") and stable event_id.
3) Legacy fields are either:
 - mapped to v1 ...
tags: Audit Logging Standardization (Schema v1 + 100% Sensitive Action Coverage)

preview: Define the canonical, versioned audit event schema (v1) for Redwood Inference across Hosted/Dedicated/Private. Deliver a JSON Schema artifact + field dictionary guidance + example events that will be used by all producers and validated by the audit-log-exporter and CI linting. This ticket is the source-of-truth for ...
text: Define the canonical, versioned audit event schema (v1) for Redwood Inference across Hosted/Dedicated/Private. Deliver a JSON Schema artifact + field dictionary guidance + example events that will be used by all producers and validated by the audit-log-exporter and CI linting. This ticket is the source-of-truth for required fields, actor/target semantics, correlation IDs, and extension rules.

title: Break-glass override: design + policy for offline bundle signature verification
preview:
Context
We are adding cryptographic signing + verification for Redwood Private offline bundles in air-gapped environments. By default we must fail closed when signature/manifest checks fail. However, pilots and regulated customers have asked for a documented “break-glass” path for emergency situations (e.g., time sk...
text:
Context
We are adding cryptographic signing + verification for Redwood Private offline bundles in air-gapped environments. By default we must fail closed when signature/manifest checks fail. However, pilots and regulated customers have asked for a documented “break-glass” path for emergency situations (e.g., time skew, trust store mismatch during key rotation, or urgent hotfix where verification cannot be performed normally).

This ticket defines:
1) Installer mechanics for break-glass override (what flags exist, what they do, what they do NOT do)
2) Guardrails/policy gating to prevent casual use
3) Audit logging requirements (local-first) + support bundle inclusion
4) Documentation + support runbooks + exception process alignment
5) Risk assessment and mitigations

Goals / Non-goals
Goals
- Provide a controlled override that is explicit, hard to invoke accidentally, and produces stro...

title: Define audit events + export behavior for offline bundle signature verification outcomes
preview:
Problem
Air-gapped / offline Redwood Private installs must cryptographically verify offline bundles (manifest + signature). Customers (especially regulated) also require an auditable record of verification outcomes, with clear failure reasons and the ability to export to SIEM when available.

Today we have a mix of ...
text:
Problem
Air-gapped / offline Redwood Private installs must cryptographically verify offline bundles (manifest + signature). Customers (especially regulated) also require an auditable record of verification outcomes, with clear failure reasons and the ability to export to SIEM when available.

Today we have a mix of installer stdout/stderr logs and ad-hoc telemetry. There is no normalized event schema that:
- is consistent across success + failure paths
- carries sufficient provenance (bundle build id, key id, algorithm, manifest digest)
- supports redaction rules (no secrets, minimal PII)
- can be exported via audit-log-exporter for customers that forward logs
- is stable across versions (schema versioning)

Goal
Define:
1) The canonical audit event names for verification-related outcomes (success/failure/break-glass).
2) A minimal stable schema (fields + semantics) with explicit reda...
tags: Redwood Private: On-prem offline artifact signing

title: Vendor Gateway: pricing-bundles experiment + SOC2 evidence handshake
preview: Create a vendor-facing onboarding gateway that couples a set of pricing/packaging experiments (bundle configurations and insurance crediting) with an automated SOC2 evidence handshake to reduce procurement friction for enterprise customers. The goal is to enable self-serve procurement for second-tier enterprise deal...
text: Create a vendor-facing onboarding gateway that couples a set of pricing/packaging experiments (bundle configurations and insurance crediting) with an automated SOC2 evidence handshake to reduce procurement friction for enterprise customers. The goal is to enable self-serve procurement for second-tier enterprise deals while surfacing needed legal/finance guardrails and collecting verifiable evidence for SOC2/insurance without repeated manual requests.

title: Quickstart landing — interactive hero with architecture strip and updated screenshot set
preview: Objective: Replace the static quickstart hero with an interactive hero that introduces the product in two layers: (1) a clean marketing-facing headline + CTA for new users and (2) an expandable architecture strip that explains the request flow (client → routing/policy → model variant → KV cache → output) for enginee...
text: Objective: Replace the static quickstart hero with an interactive hero that introduces the product in two layers: (1) a clean marketing-facing headline + CTA for new users and (2) an expandable architecture strip that explains the request flow (client → routing/policy → model variant → KV cache → output) for engineering audiences. Scope: hero composition, supporting 3 responsive breakpoints (desktop/tablet/mobile), an 'architecture strip' micro-interaction, and an updated set of product screenshots (hosted API request, dedicated capacity dashboard snapshot, private VPC connection modal). Constraints: minimal engineering effort for landing page rollout (we must limit new frontend components to an expandable strip + animated microtransitions), maintain <3s LCP for hero, ensure WCAG AA contrast and keyboard/ screen-reader accessibility for the interactive strip. Trade-offs considered: fu...

title: Security review: log redaction + retention defaults for Private observability minimum pack
preview:
Context
- This ticket is the required Security & Compliance review for the Redwood Private “observability minimum pack” logging portion.
- Goal: ensure (1) Redwood components do not emit secrets/PII into operational logs by default, (2) the default retention guidance/values shipped in Helm/installer are compliant an...
text:
Context
- This ticket is the required Security & Compliance review for the Redwood Private “observability minimum pack” logging portion.
- Goal: ensure (1) Redwood components do not emit secrets/PII into operational logs by default, (2) the default retention guidance/values shipped in Helm/installer are compliant and defensible, and (3) export integrations (Splunk HEC / Elastic/OpenSearch / Loki / OTLP where applicable) include safe defaults and clear customer responsibility boundaries.

In-scope components/log sources
- Control plane: API gateway, auth/RBAC integration points, policy/routing service, installer/upgrade controller, model registry integration service.
- Data plane: serving runtime pods, request router, batching/caching components.
- Observability plumbing: log collector config shipped in the minimum pack (baseline collector + parsing/filters), Helm values defaults.

Thr...

title: Security review: audit events + RBAC for Dedicated burst config/admin actions
preview:
Background
We are introducing Dedicated “burst capacity” (shared Redwood-managed burst pools). We must ensure all admin/config actions that affect burst behavior are auditable, permissions are least-privilege, and we have security review evidence ready for GA and for regulated customers (e.g., finance/security quest...
text:
Background
We are introducing Dedicated “burst capacity” (shared Redwood-managed burst pools). We must ensure all admin/config actions that affect burst behavior are auditable, permissions are least-privilege, and we have security review evidence ready for GA and for regulated customers (e.g., finance/security questionnaires).

Scope of this ticket
1) Audit logging coverage
- Confirm we emit audit events for all burst-related control plane changes and admin overrides.
- Ensure events contain sufficient context to answer: who did what, to which tenant, what changed, when, from where, and via which mechanism (Console/API/support tooling).
- Ensure sensitive data is not logged (no customer prompts, no request payloads, no secrets).

2) RBAC / authorization
- Validate who is allowed to:
 a) enable/disable burst for a tenant
 b) change burst limits (max burst ratio, tenant cap, cooldown ov...

title: Security review + threat model for SIEM audit-log exporter (secrets, egress/SSRF, leakage, encryption/logging)
preview:
Goal: complete Security Engineering threat model + sign-off for the SIEM-ready audit export beta (hosted + dedicated). Scope is exporter service + destination adapters (Splunk HEC, Datadog Logs, S3), control-plane config surface, secret handling, and operational surfaces.

This ticket is the gating security review f...
text:
Goal: complete Security Engineering threat model + sign-off for the SIEM-ready audit export beta (hosted + dedicated). Scope is exporter service + destination adapters (Splunk HEC, Datadog Logs, S3), control-plane config surface, secret handling, and operational surfaces.

This ticket is the gating security review for beta enablement (tenant allowlist). It should produce: (1) a concrete threat model write-up for audit export, (2) actionable mitigations tracked as follow-up issues/PRs, and (3) explicit sign-off criteria for Security + SRE.

Primary areas to review:
- Secrets handling: destination credentials (Splunk HEC token, Datadog API key, AWS role/KMS key IDs), storage, access controls, rotation, redaction in logs.
- SSRF / egress controls: prevent arbitrary outbound requests; destination validation; allowlist strategy; DNS / IP / redirect handling; private IP ranges.
- Data leaka...

title: Privacy review: onboarding checklist events are PII-free + retention/compliance aligned
preview:
Context
We are shipping onboarding/progress tracking via a standardized event schema emitted primarily server-side (gateway/control plane) to drive the Console checklist and TTFS/TTFP KPIs.

This ticket is the privacy/security validation pass to ensure:
- Event payloads are PII-free (and do not accidentally capture ...
text:
Context
We are shipping onboarding/progress tracking via a standardized event schema emitted primarily server-side (gateway/control plane) to drive the Console checklist and TTFS/TTFP KPIs.

This ticket is the privacy/security validation pass to ensure:
- Event payloads are PII-free (and do not accidentally capture customer prompts, outputs, headers, or identifiers like emails).
- Retention + residency are aligned with Redwood’s compliance posture and the existing data residency/retention guidance for onboarding telemetry.
- Guardrails exist in code and schema to prevent future regressions.

In scope
- Review onboarding event schema v1 fields + examples used by gateway/control plane + OpenAI-compat layer.
- Validate current emission points and payload construction do not contain PII.
- Confirm retention policy for raw events vs derived checklist state vs aggregated KPIs.
- Confirm pro...
tags: Guided onboarding + first production launch checklist

title: Emit audit log events for Hosted rollout actions (create/start/step/pause/approve/rollback) with actor + diffs
preview: We need GA-quality audit logging coverage for Hosted canary rollouts. Emit customer-visible, exportable audit log events for rollout create/start/step-change/pause/resume/approve/rollback/cancel actions. Events must include: tenant, environment, route identifiers, rollout_id, previous/new state, actor identity (user...
text:
We need GA-quality audit logging coverage for Hosted canary rollouts. Emit customer-visible, exportable audit log events for rollout create/start/step-change/pause/resume/approve/rollback/cancel actions. Events must include: tenant, environment, route identifiers, rollout_id, previous/new state, actor identity (user/service), request correlation IDs, and a safe diff of relevant config fields. Also emit events for system-initiated actions (e.g., auto-pause/auto-rollback on SLO breach) with an explicit system actor + reason metadata.

This is a GA exit requirement for regulated customers (change control + investigations) and is explicitly requested in security reviews.

title: Audit log events for route experiment lifecycle (create/update/start/stop) + export/retention verification
preview:
## Context
Route-level A/B testing introduces customer-visible configuration that changes request routing behavior. For enterprise customers (esp. regulated) we need complete auditability for experiment lifecycle actions (create/update/start/stop), including who changed what, when, from where, and with what resultin...
text:
## Context
Route-level A/B testing introduces customer-visible configuration that changes request routing behavior. For enterprise customers (esp. regulated) we need complete auditability for experiment lifecycle actions (create/update/start/stop), including who changed what, when, from where, and with what resulting state.

This ticket implements and validates audit events for:
- experiment.create
- experiment.update
- experiment.start
- experiment.stop

…and verifies:
- retention meets compliance expectations
- export format includes required fields for SIEM/Splunk/S3 pipelines
- redaction rules are correct (no prompts, no secrets)

Primary references:
- Confluence: security-and-compliance/audit-logging/experiment-config-audit-events.json
- GA requirements: PM-482

## Requirements / Event model
### Event names
Use the existing Redwood audit event conventions:
- resource_type: "exper...
